prompt: Using ProteinMPNN with multi-state design, generate sequence variants of epsilon toxin domain B from Clostridium perfringens type D (PDB 1UYJ pre-pore) in both pre-pore and predicted pore-inserted conformations to identify residues that control the pre-pore-to-pore transition as candidate sites for inhibitor development against epsilon toxin intoxication. outline a computational approach covering: (1) modelling of pore-inserted conformation by comparative modelling on alpha-toxin (PDB 7AHL), (2) multi-state ProteinMPNN run using pre-pore and pore models simultaneously, (3) analysis of residue frustration to identify conformational gate residues, (4) mutagenesis predictions and suggested biochemical validation in lipid-bilayer ion-conductance assays. Include discussion of select agent status and BSL-2 handling requirements for recombinant epsilon toxin fragments.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05) and RCSB PDB (1UYJ); target: Epsilon toxin domain B; organism: Clostridium perfringens type D; catalogue version v1.0

_provenance.target_class: pore-forming toxin pre-pore and pore conformational states (select agent)
